title: Add Two Numbers
description: You are given two non-empty linked lists representing two non-negative integers. The digits are stored in reverse order and each of their nodes contains a single digit. Add the two numbers and return it as a linked list. You may assume the two numbers do not contain any leading zero, except the number 0 itself. Each linked list node is represented as ListNode with a value val and a pointer to the next node next.

examples.explanation: The numbers represented by the linked lists are 342 (from l1) and 465 (from l2). Adding these gives 807, which is represented as the linked list [7,0,8].
Both numbers are 0, so the sum is also 0, represented as a single node linked list [0].
The first number is 9999999 and the second is 999. Their sum is 10009998, which is represented as the linked list [8,9,9,0,0,0,1].

constraints: Each linked list represents a non-negative integer.
The number of nodes in each linked list is at most 100.
Each node's value is between 0 and 9.
hints: Consider how to handle carrying over when the sum exceeds 9.
Use a dummy head node to simplify the result linked list construction.
Make sure to process both linked lists until all nodes are consumed.
Don't forget to handle the case where there's still a carry after processing both lists.
